title: story-16-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of story-16-review-churn. Since 2026-05-28T23:34:26.209621+00:00, story 16 completed another successful dev run followed by another successful reviewer run, and the watcher note reports the cumulative reviewer-cycle count has now risen to 8 while the story remains active in-window. There are still no failed runs or retry-storm signals; the pattern is repeated successful devreviewer ping-pong without convergence.

The immediate impact is continued cost burn and story-level delay rather than a factory-wide outage. In this window alone, the story consumed about $0.233 across the dev and reviewer runs, with the dev run taking 268.33 seconds before another reviewer pass. The evidence supports a structural non-convergence pattern rather than an infrastructure failure: work is being produced and reviewed successfully, but the story is not exiting the review loop.

Given the detector guidance, review churn at 8 active reviewer cycles is itself escalation-worthy even without explicit errors. Plausible explanations include incomplete carry-forward of prior review context or another loop-inducing prompt/dispatch behavior, but the current evidence does not isolate a single mechanism. No broader queue, tick, spend-spike, or placeholder-leak signal in this window suggests a wider factory malfunction.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Activity resumed on story 16 in this window: a successful dev run was followed by a successful reviewer run, raising its cumulative review churn to 8 reviewer cycles and leaving it actively cycling.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"Story 16 is active in this window and has risen to 8 reviewer cycles, continuing the previously escalated non-converging review loop; other high-churn stories remain parked."}
story 16 dev run succeeded at attempt 11 after 268.33s, cost_usd 0.1988575, tokens_in 386528, tokens_out 3729.
story 16 reviewer run succeeded at attempt 8 after 23.517s, cost_usd 0.03414, following the dev run and keeping the story in review.

escalation_reason: Active review-churn pattern on story 16 has persisted across multiple summarizer intervals and now stands at 8 reviewer cycles. The story continues to consume additional cost and time without converging, and L3 judgment is needed to assess prompt/loop mechanics behind the non-converging dev-review cycle.
